A.O.P. / Artisan

LMAH was established to bring a benchmark of European fine food excellence to the South African wholesale market — one that has simply not existed here before. Our founding credentials are built on decades at the highest levels of the European fine food industry.

"The standards of Munich, the provenance of France and Italy — now available to the South African trade."

Günther Abt, who leads the sourcing and curation programme, served as Managing Director of Dallmayr — the storied Munich fine food house founded in 1700. He went on to found Abt Cheese in Bavaria, now an established multi-branch operation recognised as one of the leading fine food specialists in the region.

LMAH launches with its flagship categories — fine cheeses, selected hams and charcuterie, and caviar — with a full provisions portfolio to follow.

Why Trade With LMAH

The Standard We Hold

Dallmayr Lineage

Our curation principal served as Managing Director of Dallmayr Germany. That standard — one of the most demanding in the world — defines everything we bring to market.

Direct Sourcing

Decades of direct relationships with A.O.P. producers, affineurs and cave-masters across France, Italy, Switzerland and beyond. No intermediaries.

Cold-Chain Integrity

Temperature-controlled logistics from source through to your cellar. The integrity of the product is never compromised in transit.

Trade Only

We operate exclusively at wholesale level — restaurants, hotels, delis, specialty retailers. No retail. No compromise on account standards.

The Principals

Creative Direction & Culinary Leadership

Günther Abt
Maître Fromager · Abt Cheese Bavaria
MD
Dallmayr Germany
MF
Maître Fromager

Fine Foods & Fromagerie · Bavaria

Günther Abt served for many years as Managing Director of Dallmayr — the storied Munich fine food house founded in 1700, widely regarded as one of the finest delicatessens in the world. In that role, he oversaw one of Europe's most exacting standards of food sourcing, supplier relations and trade operations.

Following that tenure, he founded Abt Cheese — now an established multi-branch fine food operation in Bavaria, recognised as one of the leading specialty cheese and provisions retailers in the region. The business is built on exceptional French and Italian cheese, with a portfolio extending to artisan pastas, truffle preparations, hams and fine pantry provisions.

LMAH is the vehicle through which this expertise, supplier network and standard of curation is introduced to South Africa — a wholesale operation built for the trade, held to a European standard.

Monticello Estate · Stellenbosch

Born in New York and raised in Italy in a big, lively household with a Spanish grandmother and a dining table that was always full, Giuliano Magnoni arrived at food the way all the best chefs do — through family, memory and an instinctive understanding of what a shared table means.

After opening a restaurant in Milan, he trained at the renowned Alma Scuola Internazionale di Cucina Italiana in Parma — Italy's most prestigious culinary institution. Today he is the resident chef and proprietor of Monticello, his estate in Stellenbosch, where the same spirit of generous, produce-led hospitality defines everything on the table.

"My style is Mediterranean and rooted in traditional, humble cuisines — from Provence and Burgundy to the Basque Country, Galicia, Emilia Romagna and Piedmont. Everything starts with great produce; if the ingredients are good, most of the work is done. The rest is respect, love and technique."

His role at LMAH bridges the culinary and the commercial — ensuring that what we bring to the South African trade is not merely sourced, but genuinely understood from the perspective of a kitchen and a table that have always placed provenance first.

Giuliano Magnoni
Chef · Monticello Estate · Stellenbosch
Alma
Parma, Italy
Chef
Monticello · Stellenbosch

Camembert de Normandie
